text.NRSV: After the death of Joshua, the Israelites inquired of the Lord, “Who shall go up first for us against the Canaanites, to fight against them?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after the death of Joshua, that the sons of Israel asked of Yahweh, saying,—Who shall go up for us against the Canaanites, first, to make war upon them?
text.KJV: 1:1 Now after the death of Ioshua, it came to passe, that the children of Israel asked the Lord, saying, Who shal goe vp for vs against the Canaanites first, to fight against them?
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. The Situation in Canaan Following the Israelite Conquest Chapter 1 - Canaanites in Palestine. After the death of Joshua the Israelites consulted the LORD, asking, “Who shall be first among us to attack the Canaanites and to do battle with them?”

text.NRSV: The Lord said, “Judah shall go up. I hereby give the land into his hand.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said—Judah, shall go,—lo! I have delivered the land into his power.
text.KJV: 1:2 And the Lord sayd, Iudah shall goe vp: Behold, I haue deliuered the land into his hand.
text.NABRE: 1:2 The LORD answered: Judah shall attack: I have delivered the land into his power.

text.NRSV: Judah said to his brother Simeon, “Come up with me into the territory allotted to me, that we may fight against the Canaanites; then I too will go with you into the territory allotted to you.” So Simeon went with him.
text.Rotherham: Then said Judah, unto Simeon his brother—Come up with me into the territory allotted me, and let us make war on the Canaanites, then will, I also, go with thee, into thy territory. So Simeon went with him.
text.KJV: 1:3 And Iudah saide vnto Simeon his brother, Come vp with me into my lot, that wee may fight against the Canaanites, and I likewise will goe with thee into thy lot. So Simeon went with him.
text.NABRE: 1:3 Judah then said to his brother Simeon, “Come up with me into the territory allotted to me, and let us do battle with the Canaanites. I will likewise go with you into the territory allotted to you.” So Simeon went with him.

text.NRSV: Then Judah went up and the Lord gave the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand; and they defeated ten thousand of them at Bezek.
text.Rotherham: And Judah went up, and Yahweh delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand,—and they smote them in Bezek, ten thousand men.
text.KJV: 1:4 And Iudah went vp, and the Lord deliuered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand: and they slew of them in Bezek ten thousand men.
text.NABRE: 1:4 When Judah attacked, the LORD delivered the Canaanites and Perizzites into their power, and they struck down ten thousand of them in Bezek.

text.NRSV: They came upon Adoni-bezek at Bezek, and fought against him, and defeated the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
text.Rotherham: And they found Adoni-bezek in Bezek, and fought with him,—and smote the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
text.KJV: 1:5 And they found Adoni-bezek in Bezek: and they fought against him, and they slew the Canaanites, and the Perizzites.
text.NABRE: 1:5 They came upon Adonibezek in Bezek and fought against him. When they struck down the Canaanites and Perizzites,

text.NRSV: Adoni-bezek fled; but they pursued him, and caught him, and cut off his thumbs and big toes.
text.Rotherham: But Adoni-bezek fled, and they pursued him,—and took him, and cut off his thumbs, and his great toes.
text.KJV: 1:6 But Adoni-bezek fled, and they pursued after him, and caught him, and cut off his thumbes, and his great toes.
text.NABRE: 1:6 Adonibezek fled. They pursued him, and when they caught him, they cut off his thumbs and big toes.

text.NRSV: Adoni-bezek said, “Seventy kings with their thumbs and big toes cut off used to pick up scraps under my table; as I have done, so God has paid me back.” They brought him to Jerusalem, and he died there.
text.Rotherham: Then said Adoni-bezek—Seventy kings, with their thumbs and great toes cut off, have been picking up crumbs under my table, as I have done, so, hath God requited me. And they brought him into Jerusalem, and he died there.
text.KJV: 1:7 And Adoni-bezek said, Threescore & ten kings, hauing their thumbs and their great toes cut off, gathered their meate vnder my table: as I haue done, so God hath requited mee, and they brought him to Ierusalem, and there he died.
text.NABRE: 1:7 “Seventy kings,” said Adonibezek, “used to pick up scraps under my table with their thumbs and big toes cut off. As I have done, so has God repaid me.” He was brought to Jerusalem, and he died there.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Judah fought against Jerusalem and took it. They put it to the sword and set the city on fire.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Judah made war upon Jerusalem, and captured it, and smote it with the edge of the sword,—and, the city, they set on fire.
text.KJV: 1:8 (Now the children of Iudah had fought against Ierusalem, and had taken it, and smitten it with the edge of the sword, and set the citie on fire)
text.NABRE: 1:8 The Judahites fought against Jerusalem, captured it, and put it to the sword, setting the city itself on fire.

text.NRSV: Afterward the people of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites who lived in the hill country, in the Negeb, and in the lowland.
text.Rotherham: And, afterwards, the sons of Judah went down to make war upon the Canaanites,—dwelling in the hill country, and in the south, and in the lowland.
text.KJV: 1:9 ¶ And afterward the children of Iudah went downe to fight against the Canaanites that dwelt in the moūtaine, & in the South, and in the valley.
text.NABRE: 1:9 Afterward the Judahites went down to fight against the Canaanites who lived in the mountain region, in the Negeb, and in the foothills.

text.NRSV: Judah went against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron (the name of Hebron was formerly Kiriath-arba); and they defeated Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai.
text.Rotherham: And Judah went against the Canaanites who were dwelling in Hebron, now, the name of Hebron, formerly, was Kiriath-arba,—and they smote Sheshai and Ahiman, and Talmai.
text.KJV: 1:10 And Iudah went against the Canaanites that dwelt in Hebron (nowe the name of Hebron before was Kiriath-arba) and they slew Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai.
text.NABRE: 1:10 Judah also marched against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron, which was formerly called Kiriath-arba, and defeated Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.

text.NRSV: From there they went against the inhabitants of Debir (the name of Debir was formerly Kiriath-sepher).
text.Rotherham: And he went from thence, against the inhabitants of Debir,—now, the name of Debir, formerly, was Kiriath-sepher.
text.KJV: 1:11 And from thence he went against the inhabitants of Debir, (& the name of Debir before was Kiriath-sepher)
text.NABRE: 1:11 They marched from there against the inhabitants of Debir, which was formerly called Kiriath-sepher.

text.NRSV: Then Caleb said, “Whoever attacks Kiriath-sepher and takes it, I will give him my daughter Achsah as wife.”
text.Rotherham: And Caleb said, He that smiteth Kiriath-sepher, and captureth it, I will give unto him Achsah my daughter, to wife.
text.KJV: 1:12 And Caleb said, Hee that smiteth Kiriath-sepher, and taketh it, to him will I giue Achsah my daughter to wife.
text.NABRE: 1:12 Caleb said, “To the man who attacks Kiriath-sepher and captures it, I will give my daughter Achsah in marriage.”

text.NRSV: And Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother, took it; and he gave him his daughter Achsah as wife.
text.Rotherham: Then Othniel, son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother, captured it,—and he gave him Achsah his daughter, to wife.
text.KJV: 1:13 And Othniel the sonne of Kenaz Calebs yonger brother tooke it: and he gaue him Achsah his daughter to wife.
text.NABRE: 1:13 Othniel captured it, the son of Caleb’s younger brother Kenaz; so Caleb gave him his daughter Achsah in marriage.

text.NRSV: When she came to him, she urged him to ask her father for a field. As she dismounted from her donkey, Caleb said to her, “What do you wish?”
text.Rotherham: And it came pass, when she came, that she moved him to ask of her father a field, and, when she alighted from off the ass, Caleb said unto her—What aileth thee?
text.KJV: 1:14 And it came to passe when shee came to him, that she moued him to aske of her father a field: and shee lighted from off her asse, and Caleb said vnto her, What wilt thou?
text.NABRE: 1:14 When she came to him, she induced him to ask her father for some land. Then, as she alighted from the donkey, Caleb asked her, “What do you want?”

text.NRSV: She said to him, “Give me a present; since you have set me in the land of the Negeb, give me also Gulloth-mayim.” So Caleb gave her Upper Gulloth and Lower Gulloth.
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him—Give me a present; for, south land, hast thou given me, give me therefore pools of water. So Caleb gave her Upper-pools, and Lower-pools.
text.KJV: 1:15 And she said vnto him, Giue me a blessing: for thou hast giuen mee a South land, giue me also springs of water. And Caleb gaue her the vpper springs, and the nether springs.
text.NABRE: 1:15 She answered, “Give me a present. Since you have put me in the land of the Negeb, give me pools of water.” So Caleb gave her what she wanted, both the upper and the lower pool.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Hobab the Kenite, Moses’ father-in-law, went up with the people of Judah from the city of palms into the wilderness of Judah, which lies in the Negeb near Arad. Then they went and settled with the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of the Kenite, father-in-law of Moses, had come up from the city of palm-trees, with the sons of Judah, into the wilderness of Judah, which is in the south of Arad,—so they went and dwelt with the people.
text.KJV: 1:16 ¶ And the children of the Kenite, Moses father in law, went vp out of the citie of palme trees, with the children of Iudah into the wildernesse of Iudah, which lieth in the South of Arad, and they went and dwelt among the people.
text.NABRE: 1:16 The descendants of Hobab the Kenite, Moses’ father-in-law, came up with the Judahites from the City of Palms to the wilderness of Arad, which is in the Negeb, and they settled among the Amalekites.

text.NRSV: Judah went with his brother Simeon, and they defeated the Canaanites who inhabited Zephath, and devoted it to destruction. So the city was called Hormah.
text.Rotherham: Then went Judah, with Simeon his brother, and they smote the Canaanites dwelling in Zephath,—and devoted it to destruction, and the name of the city was called Hormah.
text.KJV: 1:17 And Iudah went with Simeon his brother, and they slew the Canaanites that inhabited Zephath, and vtterly destroyed it, (and the name of the citie was called Hormah.)
text.NABRE: 1:17 Then Judah went with his brother Simeon, and they defeated the Canaanites who lived in Zephath. They put the city under the ban and renamed it Hormah.

text.NRSV: Judah took Gaza with its territory, Ashkelon with its territory, and Ekron with its territory.
text.Rotherham: And Judah captured Gaza with the boundaries thereof, and Ashkelon, with the boundaries thereof,—and Ekron, with the boundaries thereof.
text.KJV: 1:18 Also Iudah tooke Gaza with the coast thereof, and Askelon with the coast thereof, and Ekron with the coast thereof.
text.NABRE: 1:18 Judah captured Gaza with its territory, Ashkelon with its territory, Ekron with its territory, and Ashdod with its territory.

text.NRSV: The Lord was with Judah, and he took possession of the hill country, but could not drive out the inhabitants of the plain, because they had chariots of iron.
text.Rotherham: And it same to pass that Yahweh was with Judah, and he took possession of the hill country,—but did not dispossess the inhabitants of the vale, because they had, chariots of iron.
text.KJV: 1:19 And the Lord was with Iudah, and hee draue out the inhabitants of the mountaine, but could not driue out the inhabitants of the valley, because they had charets of yron.
text.NABRE: 1:19 The LORD was with Judah, so they gained possession of the mountain region. But they could not dispossess those who lived on the plain, because they had iron chariots.

text.NRSV: Hebron was given to Caleb, as Moses had said; and he drove out from it the three sons of Anak.
text.Rotherham: So they gave unto Caleb Hebron, as spake Moses,—and he drave out from thence the three sons of Anak.
text.KJV: 1:20 And they gaue Hebron vnto Caleb, as Moses saide: and hee expelled thence the three sonnes of Anak.
text.NABRE: 1:20 As Moses had commanded, they gave Hebron to Caleb, who then drove the three sons of Anak away from there.

text.NRSV: But the Benjaminites did not drive out the Jebusites who lived in Jerusalem; so the Jebusites have lived in Jerusalem among the Benjaminites to this day.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jebusites dwelling in Jerusalem, the sons of Benjamin did not drive out,—but the Jebusites have dwelt with the sons of Benjamin, in Jerusalem, unto this day.
text.KJV: 1:21 And the children of Beniamin did not driue out the Iebusites that inhabited Ierusalem: but the Iebusites dwel with the children of Beniamin in Ierusalem, vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 1:21 As for the Jebusites dwelling in Jerusalem, the Benjaminites did not dispossess them, so that the Jebusites live with the Benjaminites in Jerusalem to the present day.

text.NRSV: The house of Joseph also went up against Bethel; and the Lord was with them.
text.Rotherham: Then went up the house of Joseph—they also,—unto Bethel,—and, Yahweh, was with them.
text.KJV: 1:22 ¶ And the house of Ioseph, they also went vp against Bethel: and the Lord was with them.
text.NABRE: 1:22 The house of Joseph, too, went up against Bethel, and the LORD was with them.

text.NRSV: The house of Joseph sent out spies to Bethel (the name of the city was formerly Luz).
text.Rotherham: And the house of Joseph sent to spy out Bethel,—now, the name of the city, formerly, was, Luz.
text.KJV: 1:23 And the house of Ioseph sent to descrie Bethel (now the name of the citie before was Luz)
text.NABRE: 1:23 The house of Joseph reconnoitered Bethel, which formerly was called Luz.

text.NRSV: When the spies saw a man coming out of the city, they said to him, “Show us the way into the city, and we will deal kindly with you.”
text.Rotherham: So the watchers saw a man, coming forth from the city,—and they said unto him—Shew us, we pray thee, the way to get into the city, and we will deal with thee, in lovingkindness.
text.KJV: 1:24 And the spies sawe a man come forth out of the citie, and they said vnto him, Shew vs, wee pray thee, the entrance into the citie, and we will shew thee mercie.
text.NABRE: 1:24 The scouts saw a man coming out of the city and said to him, “Tell us the way into the city, and we will show you mercy.”

text.NRSV: So he showed them the way into the city; and they put the city to the sword, but they let the man and all his family go.
text.Rotherham: And he shewed them the way to get into the city, and they smote the city, with the edge of the sword,—but, the man and all his family, they let go.
text.KJV: 1:25 And when hee shewed them the entrance into the citie, they smote the citie with the edge of the sword: but they let goe the man and all his familie.
text.NABRE: 1:25 He showed them the way into the city, and they put the city to the sword; but they let the man and his whole clan go free.

text.NRSV: So the man went to the land of the Hittites and built a city, and named it Luz; that is its name to this day.
text.Rotherham: And the man went into the land of the Hittites,—and built a city, and called the name thereof, Luz, that, is the name thereof, unto this day.
text.KJV: 1:26 And the man went into the lande of the Hittites, and built a citie, and called the name thereof Luz: which is the name thereof vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 1:26 The man then went to the land of the Hittites, where he built a city and called it Luz, which is its name to this day.

text.NRSV: Manasseh did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shean and its villages, or Taanach and its villages, or the inhabitants of Dor and its villages, or the inhabitants of Ibleam and its villages, or the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages; but the Canaanites continued to live in that land.
text.Rotherham: But Manasseh took not possession of Beth-shean and her towns, nor of Taanach and her towns, nor dispossessed the inhabitants of Dor and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Ibleam, and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Megiddo, and her towns,—but the Canaanites were determined to remain in this land;
text.KJV: 1:27 ¶ Neither did Manasseh driue out the inhabitants of Bethshean, and her townes, nor Taanach and her townes, nor the inhabitants of Dor, and her townes, nor the inhabitants of Ibleam, and her townes, nor the inhabitants of Megiddo, and her townes: but the Canaanites would dwel in that land.
text.NABRE: 1:27 Manasseh did not take possession of Beth-shean with its towns or of Taanach with its towns. Nor did they dispossess the inhabitants of Dor and its towns, those of Ibleam and its towns, or those of Megiddo and its towns. The Canaanites continued to live in this district.

text.NRSV: When Israel grew strong, they put the Canaanites to forced labor, but did not in fact drive them out.
text.Rotherham: when Israel, however, had waxed strong, they put the Canaanites under tribute,—though they, dispossessed, them not.
text.KJV: 1:28 And it came to passe when Israel was strong, that they put the Canaanites to tribute, and did not vtterly driue them out.
text.NABRE: 1:28 When Israel grew stronger, they conscripted the Canaanites as laborers, but did not actually drive them out.

text.NRSV: And Ephraim did not drive out the Canaanites who lived in Gezer; but the Canaanites lived among them in Gezer.
text.Rotherham: And, Ephraim, dispossessed not the Canaanites who were dwelling in Gezer,—so the Canaanites remained in their midst, in Gezer.
text.KJV: 1:29 ¶ Neither did Ephraim driue out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer: but the Canaanites dwelt in Gezer among them.
text.NABRE: 1:29 Ephraim did not drive out the Canaanites living in Gezer, and so the Canaanites lived among them in Gezer.

text.NRSV: Zebulun did not drive out the inhabitants of Kitron, or the inhabitants of Nahalol; but the Canaanites lived among them, and became subject to forced labor.
text.Rotherham: Zebulun, dispossessed not the inhabitants of Kitron, nor the inhabitants of Nahalol,—but the Canaanites remained in their midst, and came under tribute.
text.KJV: 1:30 ¶ Neither did Zebulun driue out the inhabitants of Kitron, nor the inhabitants of Nahalol: but the Canaanites dwelt among them, and became tributaries.
text.NABRE: 1:30 Nor did Zebulun dispossess the inhabitants of Kitron or those of Nahalol; the Canaanites lived among them and became forced laborers.

text.NRSV: Asher did not drive out the inhabitants of Acco, or the inhabitants of Sidon, or of Ahlab, or of Achzib, or of Helbah, or of Aphik, or of Rehob;
text.Rotherham: Asher, dispossessed not the inhabitants of Acco, nor the inhabitants of Zidon,—nor of Ahlab, nor of Achzib, nor of Helbah, nor of Aphik, nor of Rehob;
text.KJV: 1:31 ¶ Neither did Asher driue out the inhabitants of Accho, nor the inhabitants of Zidon, nor of Ahlab, nor Achzib, nor Helbath, nor Aphik, nor of Rehob:
text.NABRE: 1:31 Nor did Asher dispossess the inhabitants of Acco or those of Sidon, or take possession of Mahaleb, Achzib, Helbah, Aphik, or Rehob.

text.NRSV: but the Asherites lived among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land; for they did not drive them out.
text.Rotherham: but the Asherites dwelt in the midst of the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land,—for they dispossessed them not.
text.KJV: 1:32 But the Asherites dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land: for they did not driue them out.
text.NABRE: 1:32 So the Asherites settled among the Canaanite inhabitants of the land, for they had not dispossessed them.

text.NRSV: Naphtali did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh, or the inhabitants of Beth-anath, but lived among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land; nevertheless the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and of Beth-anath became subject to forced labor for them.
text.Rotherham: Naphtali, dispossessed not the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh nor the inhabitants of Beth-anath, but they dwelt in the midst of the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land,—and, the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh, and of Beth-anath, became theirs, under tribute.
text.KJV: 1:33 ¶ Neither did Naphtali driue out the inhabitants of Bethshemesh, nor the inhabitants of Bethanath, but hee dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land: neuerthelesse, the inhabitants of Bethshemesh, and of Bethanath, became tributaries vnto them.
text.NABRE: 1:33 Nor did Naphtali drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh or those of Beth-anath. They settled among the Canaanite inhabitants of the land and the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath became forced laborers for them.

text.NRSV: The Amorites pressed the Danites back into the hill country; they did not allow them to come down to the plain.
text.Rotherham: And the Amorites forced the sons of Dan into the hill country,—for they suffered them not to come down into the vale;
text.KJV: 1:34 And the Amorites forced the children of Dan into the mountaine: for they would not suffer them to come downe to the valley.
text.NABRE: 1:34 The Amorites hemmed in the Danites in the mountain region, not permitting them to come down onto the plain.

text.NRSV: The Amorites continued to live in Har-heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim, but the hand of the house of Joseph rested heavily on them, and they became subject to forced labor.
text.Rotherham: and, though the Amorites were determined to remain in the hill country of Heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim, yet was the hand of the house of Joseph heavy, so that they came under tribute.
text.KJV: 1:35 But the Amorites would dwell in mount Heres in Aiialon, & in Shaalbim: yet the hand of the house of Ioseph preuailed, so that they became tributaries.
text.NABRE: 1:35 So the Amorites continued to live in Harheres, Aijalon, and Shaalbim, but as the power of the house of Joseph grew, they were conscripted as laborers.

text.NRSV: The border of the Amorites ran from the ascent of Akrabbim, from Sela and upward.
text.Rotherham: And, the boundary of the Amorites, was from the ascent of Akrabbim, from Sela and upwards.
text.KJV: 1:36 And the coast of the Amorites was from the going vp to Akrabbim, from the rocke, and vpward.
text.NABRE: 1:36 The territory of the Amorites extended from the Akrabbim pass, from Sela and upward.

text.NRSV: Now the angel of the Lord went up from Gilgal to Bochim, and said, “I brought you up from Egypt, and brought you into the land that I had promised to your ancestors. I said, ‘I will never break my covenant with you.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh came up from Gilgal, unto Bochim. And he said—I led you up out of Egypt, and brought you into the land, which I had sworn unto your fathers, And I said, I will not break my covenant with you, to times age-abiding;
text.KJV: 2:1 And an Angel of the Lord came vp from Gilgal to Bochim, and said, I made you to goe vp out of Egypt, and haue brought you vnto the land which I sware vnto your fathers, and I said, I will neuer breake my Couenant with you.
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 - Infidelities of the Israelites. A messenger of the LORD went up from Gilgal to Bochim and said, I brought you up from Egypt and led you into the land which I promised on oath to your ancestors. I said, I will never break my covenant with you,

text.NRSV: For your part, do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of this land; tear down their altars.’ But you have not obeyed my command. See what you have done!
text.Rotherham: But, ye, must not solemnise a covenant with the inhabitants of this land, Their altars, must ye tear down,—But ye have not hearkened unto my voice, what is this ye have done?
text.KJV: 2:2 And yee shall make no league with the inhabitants of this land, you shal throw downe their altars: But ye haue not obeyed my voyce; Why haue ye done this?
text.NABRE: 2:2 but you must not make a covenant with the inhabitants of this land; you must pull down their altars. But you did not listen to me. Look what you have done!

text.NRSV: So now I say, I will not drive them out before you; but they shall become adversaries to you, and their gods shall be a snare to you.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore also I said, I will not drive them out from before you,—but they shall be your adversaries, and, their gods, shall become unto you a snare.
text.KJV: 2:3 Wherefore I also said, I will not driue them out from before you: but they shalbe as thornes in your sides, and their gods shalbe a snare vnto you.
text.NABRE: 2:3 For I also said, I will not clear them out of your way; they will become traps for you, and their gods a snare for you.

text.NRSV: When the angel of the Lord spoke these words to all the Israelites, the people lifted up their voices and wept.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the messenger of Yahweh had spoken these words unto all the sons of Israel, that the people lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.KJV: 2:4 And it came to passe when the Angel of the Lord spake these words vnto all the children of Israel, that the people lift vp their voice, and wept.
text.NABRE: 2:4 When the messenger of the LORD had spoken these things to all the Israelites, the people wept aloud.

text.NRSV: So they named that place Bochim, and there they sacrificed to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So they called the name of that place, Bochim, and offered sacrifice there, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 2:5 And they called the name of that place Bochim: and they sacrificed there vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 2:5 They named that place Bochim, and they offered sacrifice there to the LORD.

text.NRSV: When Joshua dismissed the people, the Israelites all went to their own inheritances to take possession of the land.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Joshua had let the people go, the sons of Israel went their way, every man to his inheritance, to take possession of the land.
text.KJV: 2:6 ¶ And when Ioshua had let the people goe, the children of Israel went euery man vnto his inheritance, to possesse the land.
text.NABRE: 2:6 Then Joshua dismissed the people, and the Israelites went, each to their own heritage, to take possession of the land.

text.NRSV: The people worshiped the Lord all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great work that the Lord had done for Israel.
text.Rotherham: And the people served Yahweh all the days of Joshua,—and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great work of Yahweh which he had wrought for Israel.
text.KJV: 2:7 And the people serued the Lord all the dayes of Ioshua, and all the dayes of the Elders that outliued Ioshua, who had seene all the great workes of the Lord, that hee did for Israel.
text.NABRE: 2:7 The people served the LORD during the entire lifetime of Joshua, and of those elders who outlived Joshua and who had seen all the great work the LORD had done for Israel.

text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun, the servant of the Lord, died at the age of one hundred ten years.
text.Rotherham: So then Joshua, the son of Nun, the servant of Yahweh, died,—being a hundred and ten years old.
text.KJV: 2:8 And Ioshua the sonne of Nun, the seruant of the Lord died, being an hundred and ten yeeres old.
text.NABRE: 2:8 Joshua, son of Nun, the servant of the LORD, died at the age of a hundred and ten,

text.NRSV: So they buried him within the bounds of his inheritance in Timnath-heres, in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
text.Rotherham: And they buried him within the bounds of his inheritance, in Timnath-heres, in the hill country of Ephraim,—on the north of Mount Gaash.
text.KJV: 2:9 And they buried him in the border of his inheritance in Timnath-Heres, in the mount of Ephraim, on the North side of the hill Gaash.
text.NABRE: 2:9 and they buried him within the borders of his heritage at Timnath-heres in the mountain region of Ephraim north of Mount Gaash.

text.NRSV: Moreover, that whole generation was gathered to their ancestors, and another generation grew up after them, who did not know the Lord or the work that he had done for Israel.
text.Rotherham: All that generation also, were gathered unto their fathers,—and there arose another generation, after them, who had not known Yahweh, nor even the work which he had wrought for Israel.
text.KJV: 2:10 And also all that generation were gathered vnto their fathers: and there arose another generation after them, which knew not the Lord, nor yet the woorkes which hee had done for Israel.
text.NABRE: 2:10 When the rest of that generation were also gathered to their ancestors, and a later generation arose that did not know the LORD or the work he had done for Israel,

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord and worshiped the Baals;
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Israel did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh,—and served the Baals;
text.KJV: 2:11 ¶ And the children of Israel did euil in the sight of the Lord, and serued Baalim:
text.NABRE: 2:11 the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the LORD. They served the Baals,

text.NRSV: and they abandoned the Lord, the God of their ancestors, who had brought them out of the land of Egypt; they followed other gods, from among the gods of the peoples who were all around them, and bowed down to them; and they provoked the Lord to anger.
text.Rotherham: and forsook Yahweh, the God of their fathers, who brought them up out of the land of Egypt, and went after other gods, from among the gods of the peoples who were round about them, and bowed themselves down unto them,—and angered Yahweh.
text.KJV: 2:12 And they forsooke the Lord God of their fathers, which brought them out of the land of Egypt, and followed other gods, of the gods of the people that were round about them, and bowed themselues vnto them, and prouoked the Lord to anger.
text.NABRE: 2:12 and abandoned the LORD, the God of their ancestors, the one who had brought them out of the land of Egypt. They followed other gods, the gods of the peoples around them, and bowed down to them, and provoked the LORD.

text.NRSV: They abandoned the Lord, and worshipped Baal and the Astartes.
text.Rotherham: Yea, they forsook Yahweh,—and did service unto the Baal and unto Ashtaroth.
text.KJV: 2:13 And they forsooke the Lord, and serued Baal and Ashtaroth.
text.NABRE: 2:13 Because they had abandoned the LORD and served Baal and the Astartes,

text.NRSV: So the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he gave them over to plunderers who plundered them, and he sold them into the power of their enemies all around, so that they could no longer withstand their enemies.
text.Rotherham: Then kindled the anger of Yahweh upon Israel, and he delivered them into the hand of spoilers, who plundered them,—and he sold them into the hand of their enemies round about, so that they could no longer stand before their enemies.
text.KJV: 2:14 ¶ And the anger of the Lord was hote against Israel, and he deliuered them into the hands of spoilers that spoiled them, and he sold them into the hands of their enemies round about, so that they could not any longer stand before their enemies.
text.NABRE: 2:14 the anger of the LORD flared up against Israel, and he delivered them into the power of plunderers who despoiled them. He sold them into the power of the enemies around them, and they were no longer able to withstand their enemies.

text.NRSV: Whenever they marched out, the hand of the Lord was against them to bring misfortune, as the Lord had warned them and sworn to them; and they were in great distress.
text.Rotherham: Whithersoever they went out, the hand of Yahweh was found to be against them for misfortune, as spake Yahweh, and as sware Yahweh unto them,—and they were distressed exceedingly.
text.KJV: 2:15 Whither soeuer they went out, the hand of the Lord was against them for euill, as the Lord had said, and as the Lord had sworne vnto them: and they were greatly distressed.
text.NABRE: 2:15 Whenever they marched out, the hand of the LORD turned against them, as the LORD had said, and as the LORD had sworn to them; and they were in great distress.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord raised up judges, who delivered them out of the power of those who plundered them.
text.Rotherham: And, though Yahweh raised up judges,—who saved them out of the hand of them that plundered them,
text.KJV: 2:16 ¶ Neuerthelesse, the Lord raysed vp Iudges, which deliuered them out of the hand of those that spoyled them.
text.NABRE: 2:16 But the LORD raised up judges to save them from the power of their plunderers;

text.NRSV: Yet they did not listen even to their judges; for they lusted after other gods and bowed down to them. They soon turned aside from the way in which their ancestors had walked, who had obeyed the commandments of the Lord; they did not follow their example.
text.Rotherham: yet, even unto their judges, did thy not hearken, for they went unchastely astray after other gods, and bowed themselves down to them,—they turned aside speedily out of the way wherein their fathers who hearkened unto the commandments of Yahweh, walked, they did not so.
text.KJV: 2:17 And yet they would not hearken vnto their Iudges, but they went a whoring after other gods, and bowed themselues vnto them: they turned quickly out of the way, which their fathers walked in, obeying the Commandements of the Lord; but they did not so.
text.NABRE: 2:17 but they did not listen to their judges either, for they prostituted themselves by following other gods, bowing down to them. They were quick to stray from the way their ancestors had taken, who obeyed the commandments of the LORD; but these did not.

text.NRSV: Whenever the Lord raised up judges for them, the Lord was with the judge, and he delivered them from the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge; for the Lord would be moved to pity by their groaning because of those who persecuted and oppressed them.
text.Rotherham: And, when Yahweh raised them up judges, then was Yahweh with the judges, and saved them out of the hand of their enemies, all the days of the judge,—for Yahweh was grieved at their outcry, because of them who oppressed them, and ill-treated them.
text.KJV: 2:18 And when the Lord raised them vp Iudges, then the Lord was with the Iudge, and deliuered them out of the hand of their enemies, all the dayes of the Iudge (for it repented the Lord, because of their gronings by reason of them that oppressed them, and vexed them:)
text.NABRE: 2:18 When the LORD raised up judges for them, he would be with the judge and save them from the power of their enemies as long as the judge lived. The LORD would change his mind when they groaned in their affliction under their oppressors.

text.NRSV: But whenever the judge died, they would relapse and behave worse than their ancestors, following other gods, worshiping them and bowing down to them. They would not drop any of their practices or their stubborn ways.
text.Rotherham: But, when the judge was dead, they again broke faith more than their fathers, by going their way after other gods, by serving them, and bowing themselves down to them,—they ceased not from their doings nor from their stubborn way.
text.KJV: 2:19 And it came to passe when the Iudge was dead, that they returned, and corrupted themselues more then their fathers, in following other gods, to serue them, and to bow downe vnto them: they ceased not from their owne doings, nor from their stubborne way.
text.NABRE: 2:19 But when the judge died, they would again do worse than their ancestors, following other gods, serving and bowing down to them, relinquishing none of their evil practices or stubborn ways.

text.NRSV: So the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel; and he said, “Because this people have transgressed my covenant that I commanded their ancestors, and have not obeyed my voice,
text.Rotherham: So then the anger of Yahweh kindled upon Israel, and he said—Forasmuch as this nation have transgressed my covenant, which I commanded their fathers, and have not hearkened unto my voice,
text.KJV: 2:20 ¶ And the anger of the Lord was hote against Israel, and he said, Because that this people hath transgressed my Couenant which I commanded their fathers, and haue not hearkened vnto my voice:
text.NABRE: 2:20 The anger of the LORD flared up against Israel, and he said: Because this nation has transgressed my covenant, which I enjoined on their ancestors, and has not listened to me,

text.NRSV: I will no longer drive out before them any of the nations that Joshua left when he died.”
text.Rotherham: I also, will no further dispossess a man from before them,—from among the nations which Joshua left remaining, when he died;
text.KJV: 2:21 I also will not henceforth driue out any from before them of the nations which Ioshua left when he died:
text.NABRE: 2:21 I for my part will not clear away for them any more of the nations Joshua left when he died.

text.NRSV: In order to test Israel, whether or not they would take care to walk in the way of the Lord as their ancestors did,
text.Rotherham: that I may, by them, put Israel to the proof,—whether they are going to be observant of the way of Yahweh, to walk therein as their fathers observed it, or not.
text.KJV: 2:22 That through them I may proue Israel, whether they will keepe the way of the Lord, to walke therein, as their fathers did keepe it, or not.
text.NABRE: 2:22 They will be made to test Israel, to see whether or not they will keep to the way of the LORD and continue in it as their ancestors did.

text.NRSV: the Lord had left those nations, not driving them out at once, and had not handed them over to Joshua.
text.Rotherham: Thus then Yahweh left these nations, not dispossessing them speedily,—neither had he delivered them into the hand of Joshua
text.KJV: 2:23 Therefore the Lord left those nations, without driuing them out hastily, neither deliuered he them into the hand of Ioshua.
text.NABRE: 2:23 Therefore the LORD allowed these nations to remain instead of expelling them immediately. He had not delivered them into the power of Joshua.

text.NRSV: Now these are the nations that the Lord left to test all those in Israel who had no experience of any war in Canaan
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the nations which Yahweh left, that he might, by them, put Israel to the proof,—all who had not known any of the wars of Canaan;
text.KJV: 3:1 Now these are the nations which the Lord left, to prooue Israel by them, (euen as many of Israel as had not knowen all the warres of Canaan;
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 - These are the nations the LORD allowed to remain, so that through them he might test Israel, all those who had not experienced any of the Canaanite wars—

text.NRSV: (it was only that successive generations of Israelites might know war, to teach those who had no experience of it before):
text.Rotherham: that the generations of the sons of Israel might certainly get to know, by being taught to make war,—such, at least, as aforetime knew nothing thereof:—
text.KJV: 3:2 Onely that the generations of the children of Israel might know to teach them warre, at the least such as before knew nothing thereof:)
text.NABRE: 3:2 to teach warfare to those generations of Israelites who had never experienced it:

text.NRSV: the five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites who lived on Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon as far as Lebo-hamath.
text.Rotherham: five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Zidonians, and the Hivites dwelling in Mount Lebanon,—from Mount Baal-hermon as far as the entering in of Hamath.
text.KJV: 3:3 Namely fiue lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hiuites that dwelt in mount Lebanon, from mount Baal-Hermon, vnto the entring in of Hamath.
text.NABRE: 3:3 the five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, the Sidonians, and the Hivites who lived in the mountain region of the Lebanon between Baal-hermon and Lebo-hamath.

text.NRSV: They were for the testing of Israel, to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of the Lord, which he commanded their ancestors by Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then [these] were [left], that, by them, he might put Israel to the proof,—to know whether they would hearken unto the commandments of Yahweh which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
text.KJV: 3:4 And they were to prooue Israel by them, to know whether they would hearken vnto the Commandements of the Lord, which hee commaunded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: 3:4 These served as a test for Israel, to know whether they would obey the commandments the LORD had enjoined on their ancestors through Moses.

text.NRSV: So the Israelites lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites;
text.Rotherham: And thus, the sons of Israel, dwelt in the midst of the Canaanites,—the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites;
text.KJV: 3:5 ¶ And the children of Israel dwelt among the Canaanites, Hittites, and Amorites, and Perizzites, and Hiuites, and Iebusites,
text.NABRE: 3:5 So the Israelites settled among the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.

text.NRSV: and they took their daughters as wives for themselves, and their own daughters they gave to their sons; and they worshiped their gods.
text.Rotherham: and took their daughters to be their wives, their own daughters moreover, gave they unto their sons, and they served their gods.
text.KJV: 3:6 And they tooke their daughters to be their wiues, and gaue their daughters to their sonnes, and serued their gods.
text.NABRE: 3:6 They took their daughters in marriage, and gave their own daughters to their sons in marriage, and served their gods.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, forgetting the Lord their God, and worshiping the Baals and the Asherahs.
text.Rotherham: Thus did the sons of Israel the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh, and forgat Yahweh their God,—and served the Baals and the Asherahs.
text.KJV: 3:7 And the children of Israel did euill in the sight of the Lord, and forgate the Lord their God, and serued Baalim, and the groues.
text.NABRE: 3:7 II. Stories of the Judges - Othniel. Then the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the LORD; they forgot the LORD, their God, and served the Baals and the Asherahs,

text.NRSV: Therefore the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of King Cushan-rishathaim of Aram-naharaim; and the Israelites served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
text.Rotherham: Then kindled the anger of Yahweh upon Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushan-rishathaim, king of Mesopotamia,—and the sons of Israel served Chushan-rishathaim, eight years.
text.KJV: 3:8 ¶ Therefore the anger of the Lord was hote against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushan-Rishathaim king of Mesopotamia: and the children of Israel serued Chushan-Rishathaim eight yeeres.
text.NABRE: 3:8 and the anger of the LORD flared up against them. He sold them into the power of Cushan-rishathaim, king of Aram Naharaim; and the Israelites served Cushan-rishathaim for eight years.

text.NRSV: But when the Israelites cried out to the Lord, the Lord raised up a deliverer for the Israelites, who delivered them, Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh, so Yahweh raised up a saviour unto the sons of Israel, who saved them,—even Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
text.KJV: 3:9 And when the children of Israel cryed vnto the Lord, the Lord raised vp a deliuerer to the children of Israel, who deliuered them, euen Othniel the sonne of Kenaz, Calebs yonger brother.
text.NABRE: 3:9 But when the Israelites cried out to the LORD, he raised up a savior for them, to save them. It was Othniel, son of Caleb’s younger brother Kenaz.

text.NRSV: The spirit of the Lord came upon him, and he judged Israel; he went out to war, and the Lord gave King Cushan-rishathaim of Aram into his hand; and his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit of Yahweh came upon him, and he judged Israel, and went out to war, and Yahweh delivered into his hand, Chushan-rishathaim, king of Syria,—and his hand prevailed over Chushan-rishathaim.
text.KJV: 3:10 And the Spirit of the Lord came vpon him, and he iudged Israel, and went out to warre, & the Lord deliuered Chushan-Rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand; and his hand preuailed against Chushan-Rishathaim.
text.NABRE: 3:10 The spirit of the LORD came upon him, and he judged Israel. When he marched out to war, the LORD delivered Cushan-rishathaim, king of Aram, into his power, and his hold on Cushan-rishathaim was firm.

text.NRSV: So the land had rest forty years. Then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
text.Rotherham: And the land had rest forty years,—and Othniel son of Kenaz, died.
text.KJV: 3:11 And the land had rest forty yeres: and Othniel the sonne of Kenaz died.
text.NABRE: 3:11 So the land was at rest for forty years, until Othniel, son of Kenaz, died.

text.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; and the Lord strengthened King Eglon of Moab against Israel, because they had done what was evil in the sight of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel again did the thing that was wicked, in the sight of Yahweh,—and Yahweh emboldened Eglon, king of Moab, against Israel, because they did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh;
text.KJV: 3:12 ¶ And the children of Israel did euill againe in the sight of the Lord: and the Lord strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had done euill in the sight of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 3:12 Ehud. Again the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the LORD, so he strengthened Eglon, king of Moab, against Israel because they did what was evil in the sight of the LORD.

text.NRSV: In alliance with the Ammonites and the Amalekites, he went and defeated Israel; and they took possession of the city of palms.
text.Rotherham: and he gathered unto him, the sons of Ammon, and Amalek,—and came and smote Israel, and took possession of the city of palm-trees.
text.KJV: 3:13 And hee gathered vnto him the children of Ammon, and Amalek, and went and smote Israel, and possessed the city of Palme-trees.
text.NABRE: 3:13 Taking the Ammonites and Amalek as allies, he went and defeated Israel, taking possession of the City of Palms.

text.NRSV: So the Israelites served King Eglon of Moab eighteen years.
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Israel served Eglon king of Moab, eighteen years.
text.KJV: 3:14 So the children of Israel serued Eglon the King of Moab eighteene yeeres.
text.NABRE: 3:14 So the Israelites served Eglon, king of Moab, for eighteen years.

text.NRSV: But when the Israelites cried out to the Lord, the Lord raised up for them a deliverer, Ehud son of Gera, the Benjaminite, a left-handed man. The Israelites sent tribute by him to King Eglon of Moab.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh, and Yahweh raised up unto them a saviour, Ehud son of Gera, a Benjamite, a man left-handed,—and the sons of Israel sent, by his hand, a present, unto Eglon king of Moab.
text.KJV: 3:15 But when the children of Israel cried vnto the Lord, the Lord raised them vp a deliuerer, Ehud the sonne of Gera, a Beniamite, a man left handed: and by him the children of Israel sent a Present vnto Eglon the king of Moab.
text.NABRE: 3:15 But when the Israelites cried out to the LORD, he raised up for them a savior, Ehud, son of Gera, a Benjaminite who was left-handed. The Israelites would send their tribute to Eglon, king of Moab, by him.

text.NRSV: Ehud made for himself a sword with two edges, a cubit in length; and he fastened it on his right thigh under his clothes.
text.Rotherham: So Ehud made himself a sword which had two edges, a cubit in length,—and girded it under his raiment, upon his right thigh.
text.KJV: 3:16 But Ehud made him a dagger (which had two edges) of a cubite length, and he did gird it vnder his raiment, vpon his right thigh,
text.NABRE: 3:16 Ehud made himself a two-edged dagger a foot long, and strapped it under his clothes on his right thigh.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the tribute to King Eglon of Moab. Now Eglon was a very fat man.
text.Rotherham: So he offered the present unto Eglon, king of Moab,—now, Eglon, was an exceedingly fat man.
text.KJV: 3:17 And he brought the present vnto Eglon king of Moab: and Eglon was a very fat man.
text.NABRE: 3:17 He presented the tribute to Eglon, king of Moab; now Eglon was a very fat man.

text.NRSV: When Ehud had finished presenting the tribute, he sent the people who carried the tribute on their way.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when he had made an end of offering the present, that he sent away the people who had been bearing the present;
text.KJV: 3:18 And when he had made an end to offer the Present, he sent away the people that bare the Present.
text.NABRE: 3:18 When he had finished presenting the tribute, he dismissed the troops who had carried the tribute.

text.NRSV: But he himself turned back at the sculptured stones near Gilgal, and said, “I have a secret message for you, O king.” So the king said, “Silence!” and all his attendants went out from his presence.
text.Rotherham: but, he himself, turned back from the images that were by Gilgal, and said, A secret word, have I, unto thee, O king! And he said—Silence! Thereupon went out from his presence all who had been standing near him.
text.KJV: 3:19 But hee himselfe turned againe from the quarries that were by Gilgal, and said, I haue a secret errand vnto thee, O king: who said, Keepe silence. And all that stood by him, went out from him.
text.NABRE: 3:19 But he himself turned back at the sculptured stones near Gilgal, and said, “I have a secret message for you, O king.” And the king said, “Silence!” Then when all his attendants had left his presence,

text.NRSV: Ehud came to him, while he was sitting alone in his cool roof chamber, and said, “I have a message from God for you.” So he rose from his seat.
text.Rotherham: And, Ehud, came in unto him, he, having been sitting in a summer parlour, which he had for himself, alone, and Ehud said, A divine word, have I, unto thee. So he arose from off his seat.
text.KJV: 3:20 And Ehud came vnto him, and he was sitting in a Summer parlour, which hee had for himselfe alone: And Ehud said, I haue a message from God vnto thee. And he arose out of his seat.
text.NABRE: 3:20 Ehud went in to him where he sat alone in his cool upper room. Ehud said, “I have a word from God for you.” So the king rose from his throne.

text.NRSV: Then Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into Eglon’s belly;
text.Rotherham: And Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the sword from off his right thigh,—and thrust it into his body;
text.KJV: 3:21 And Ehud put forth his left hand, and tooke the dagger from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly.
text.NABRE: 3:21 Then Ehud with his left hand drew the dagger from his right thigh, and thrust it into Eglon’s belly.

text.NRSV: the hilt also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not draw the sword out of his belly; and the dirt came out.
text.Rotherham: and, the handle also, went in after the blade, and the flesh closed upon the blade, for he withdrew not the sword out of his body,—and he came out into the ante-chamber.
text.KJV: 3:22 And the haft also went in after the blade: and the fatte closed vpon the blade, so that hee could not drawe the dagger out of his belly, and the dirt came out.
text.NABRE: 3:22 The hilt also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade because he did not withdraw the dagger from the body.

text.NRSV: Then Ehud went out into the vestibule, and closed the doors of the roof chamber on him, and locked them.
text.Rotherham: And, when Ehud came out into the porch, he closed the doors of the parlour upon him, and bolted them.
text.KJV: 3:23 Then Ehud went forth through the porche, and shut the doores of the parlour vpon him, and locked them.
text.NABRE: 3:23 Then Ehud went out onto the porch, shutting the doors of the upper room on Eglon and locking them.

text.NRSV: After he had gone, the servants came. When they saw that the doors of the roof chamber were locked, they thought, “He must be relieving himself in the cool chamber.”
text.Rotherham: When, he, had come out, his servants, went in, and looked, and lo! the doors of the parlour were bolted,—so they said, Surely he covereth his feet, in the summer chamber.
text.KJV: 3:24 When he was gone out, his seruants came, and when they saw, that behold, the doores of the parlour were locked, they said, Surely he couereth his feet in his Summer chamber.
text.NABRE: 3:24 When Ehud had left and the servants had come, they saw that the doors of the upper room were locked, and thought, “He must be easing himself in the cool chamber.”

text.NRSV: So they waited until they were embarrassed. When he still did not open the doors of the roof chamber, they took the key and opened them. There was their lord lying dead on the floor.
text.Rotherham: But, though they tarried a long time, yet lo! he opened not the doors of the parlour,—so they took the key, and opened [them], when lo! their lord, fallen to the ground dead.
text.KJV: 3:25 And they taried till they were ashamed: and behold, he opened not the doores of the parlour, therefore they tooke a key, and opened them: and behold, their lord was fallen downe dead on the earth.
text.NABRE: 3:25 They waited until they were at a loss when he did not open the doors of the upper room. So they took the key and opened them, and there was their lord lying on the floor, dead.

text.NRSV: Ehud escaped while they delayed, and passed beyond the sculptured stones, and escaped to Seirah.
text.Rotherham: But, Ehud, escaped while they delayed,—yea, he, passed the images, and escaped into Seirah.
text.KJV: 3:26 And Ehud escaped while they taried: and passed beyond the quarries, and escaped vnto Seirath.
text.NABRE: 3:26 During their delay Ehud escaped and, passing the sculptured stones, took refuge in Seirah.

text.NRSV: When he arrived, he sounded the trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim; and the Israelites went down with him from the hill country, having him at their head.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when he came, that he blew with a horn, throughout the hill country of Ephraim,—and the sons of Israel came down with him, out of the hill country, he, being before them.
text.KJV: 3:27 And it came to passe when hee was come, that hee blew a trumpet in the mountaine of Ephraim, and the children of Israel went downe with him from the mount, & he before them.
text.NABRE: 3:27 On his arrival he sounded the horn in the mountain region of Ephraim, and the Israelites went down from the mountains with him as their leader.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Follow after me; for the Lord has given your enemies the Moabites into your hand.” So they went down after him, and seized the fords of the Jordan against the Moabites, and allowed no one to cross over.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Come down after me, for Yahweh hath delivered your enemies, the Moabites, into your hand. So they came down after him, and captured the fords of the Jordan, towards Moab, and suffered not a man to pass over.
text.KJV: 3:28 And hee said vnto them, Follow after me: for the Lord hath deliuered your enemies the Moabites into your hand. And they went downe after him, and tooke the foords of Iordan toward Moab, and suffered not a man to passe ouer.
text.NABRE: 3:28 “Follow me,” he said to them, “for the LORD has delivered your enemies the Moabites into your power.” So they followed him down and seized the fords of the Jordan against the Moabites, permitting no one to cross.

text.NRSV: At that time they killed about ten thousand of the Moabites, all strong, able-bodied men; no one escaped.
text.Rotherham: And they smote of Moab, at that time, about ten thousand men, every one a mighty man, and every one a man of valour,—and, there escaped not a man.
text.KJV: 3:29 And they slewe of Moab at that time about ten thousand men, all lusty, and all men of valour, and there escaped not a man.
text.NABRE: 3:29 On that occasion they slew about ten thousand Moabites, all of them strong warriors. Not one escaped.

text.NRSV: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years.
text.Rotherham: And Moab was subdued on that day, under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years, and Ehud judged them until his death.
text.KJV: 3:30 So Moab was subdued that day vnder the hand of Israel: and the land had rest fourescore yeeres.
text.NABRE: 3:30 So Moab was brought under the power of Israel at that time; and the land had rest for eighty years.

text.NRSV: After him came Shamgar son of Anath, who killed six hundred of the Philistines with an oxgoad. He too delivered Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, was Shamgar, son of Anath, who smote of the Philistines, six hundred men, with an ox-goad,—and, he also, saved Israel.
text.KJV: 3:31 ¶ And after him was Shamgar the sonne of Anath, which slew of the Philistines sixe hundred men with an oxe goad, and he also deliuered Israel.
text.NABRE: 3:31 Shamgar. After him there was Shamgar, son of Anath, who slew six hundred Philistines with an oxgoad. He, too, was a savior for Israel.

text.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, after Ehud died.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel again did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh,—when, Ehud, was dead.
text.KJV: 4:1 And the children of Israel againe did euil in the sight of the Lord, when Ehud was dead.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 - Deborah and Barak. The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the LORD; Ehud was dead.

text.NRSV: So the Lord sold them into the hand of King Jabin of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor; the commander of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-ha-goiim.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor,—now, the prince of his host, was Sisera, and, he, dwelt in Harosheth of the nations.
text.KJV: 4:2 And the Lord sold them into the hande of Iabin king of Canaan: that reigned in Hazor, the captaine of whose host was Sisera, which dwelt in Harosheth of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: 4:2 So the LORD sold them into the power of the Canaanite king, Jabin, who reigned in Hazor. The general of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-ha-goiim.

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites cried out to the Lord for help; for he had nine hundred chariots of iron, and had oppressed the Israelites cruelly twenty years.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh,—for he had, nine hundred chariots of iron, and, he, oppressed the sons of Israel heavily, twenty years.
text.KJV: 4:3 And the children of Israel cried vnto the Lord: for he had nine hundred charets of yron: and twentie yeres hee mightily oppressed the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 4:3 But the Israelites cried out to the LORD; for with his nine hundred iron chariots Jabin harshly oppressed the Israelites for twenty years.

text.NRSV: At that time Deborah, a prophetess, wife of Lappidoth, was judging Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, Deborah, a woman who was a prophetess, wife of Lapidoth, she, was judging Israel, at that time:
text.KJV: 4:4 ¶ And Deborah a prophetesse, the wife of Lapidoth, shee iudged Israel at that time.
text.NABRE: 4:4 At that time the prophet Deborah, wife of Lappidoth, was judging Israel.

text.NRSV: She used to sit under the palm of Deborah between Ramah and Bethel in the hill country of Ephraim; and the Israelites came up to her for judgment.
text.Rotherham: she used to sit, therefore, under the palm-tree of Deborah, between Ramah and Bethel, in the hill country of Ephraim,—and the sons of Israel came up to her, for justice.
text.KJV: 4:5 And shee dwelt vnder the palme tree of Deborah, betweene Ramah and Bethel in mount Ephraim: and the children of Israel came vp to her for iudgement.
text.NABRE: 4:5 She used to sit under Deborah’s palm tree, between Ramah and Bethel in the mountain region of Ephraim, where the Israelites came up to her for judgment.

text.NRSV: She sent and summoned Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali, and said to him, “The Lord, the God of Israel, commands you, ‘Go, take position at Mount Tabor, bringing ten thousand from the tribe of Naphtali and the tribe of Zebulun.
text.Rotherham: And she sent and called for Barak son of Abinoam, out of Kadesh-naphtali,—and said unto him—Hath not Yahweh God of Israel, commanded,—Come and draw towards Mount Tabor, and bring with thee—ten thousand men, of the sons of Naphtali, and of the sons of Zebulun;
text.KJV: 4:6 And shee sent and called Barak the sonne of Abinoam, out of Kedesh-Naphtali, and said vnto him, Hath not the Lord God of Israel commaunded, saying, Goe, and drawe toward mount Tabor, and take with thee ten thousand men of the children of Naphtali, and of the children of Zebulun?
text.NABRE: 4:6 She had Barak, son of Abinoam, summoned from Kedesh of Naphtali. She said to him, “This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, commands: Go, march against Mount Tabor, and take with you ten thousand men from Naphtali and Zebulun.

text.NRSV: I will draw out Sisera, the general of Jabin’s army, to meet you by the Wadi Kishon with his chariots and his troops; and I will give him into your hand.’”
text.Rotherham: and I will draw unto thee, unto the torrent of Kishon, Sisera, prince of the host of Jabin, with his chariots, and with his multitude,—and will deliver him into thy hand?
text.KJV: 4:7 And I wil draw vnto thee to the riuer Kishon, Sisera the captaine of Iabins army, with his charets, and his multitude, and I will deliuer him into thine hand.
text.NABRE: 4:7 I will draw Sisera, the general of Jabin’s army, out to you at the Wadi Kishon, together with his chariots and troops, and I will deliver them into your power.”

text.NRSV: Barak said to her, “If you will go with me, I will go; but if you will not go with me, I will not go.”
text.Rotherham: And Barak said unto her, If thou wilt go with me, then will I go—but, if thou wilt not go with me, I will not go.
text.KJV: 4:8 And Barak said vnto her, If thou wilt goe with me, then I wil goe: but if thou wilt not goe with mee, then I will not goe.
text.NABRE: 4:8 But Barak answered her, “If you come with me, I will go; if you do not come with me, I will not go.”

text.NRSV: And she said, “I will surely go with you; nevertheless, the road on which you are going will not lead to your glory, for the Lord will sell Sisera into the hand of a woman.” Then Deborah got up and went with Barak to Kedesh.
text.Rotherham: And she said—I will, go, with thee; only, it shall not be, thine own honour, that shall arise from the journey which thou art about to take, for, into the hand of a woman, will Yahweh sell Sisera. So Deborah arose, and went with Barak, towards Kadesh.
text.KJV: 4:9 And she said, I wil surely go with thee, notwithstanding the iourney that thou takest, shal not be for thine honor: for the Lord shall sell Sisera into the hand of a woman. And Deborah arose, & went with Barak to Kedesh.
text.NABRE: 4:9 “I will certainly go with you,” she replied, “but you will not gain glory for the expedition on which you are setting out, for it is into a woman’s power that the LORD is going to sell Sisera.” So Deborah arose and went with Barak and journeyed with him to Kedesh.

text.NRSV: Barak summoned Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh; and ten thousand warriors went up behind him; and Deborah went up with him.
text.Rotherham: And Barak called together Zebulun and Naphtali, towards Kadesh, and there went up at his feet—ten thousand men,—and Deborah, went up with him.
text.KJV: 4:10 ¶ And Barak called Zebulun, and Naphtali to Kedesh, and he went vp with ten thousand men at his feete: and Deborah went vp with him.
text.NABRE: 4:10 Barak summoned Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh, and ten thousand men followed him. Deborah also went up with him.

text.NRSV: Now Heber the Kenite had separated from the other Kenites, that is, the descendants of Hobab the father-in-law of Moses, and had encamped as far away as Elon-bezaanannim, which is near Kedesh.
text.Rotherham: Now, Heber the Kenite, had separated himself from the Kenites, even from the sons of Hobab, father-in-law of Moses,—and moved his tent as far as the oak of Zaanaim, which is near Kadesh.
text.KJV: 4:11 Now Heber the Kenite, which was of the children of Hobab the father in law of Moses, had seuered himselfe from the Kenites, and pitched his tent vnto the plaine of Zaanaim, which is by Kedesh.
text.NABRE: 4:11 Now Heber the Kenite had detached himself from Cain, the descendants of Hobab, Moses’ father-in-law, and had pitched his tent by the terebinth of Zaanannim, which was near Kedesh.

text.NRSV: When Sisera was told that Barak son of Abinoam had gone up to Mount Tabor,
text.Rotherham: And they told Sisera that Barak, son of Abinoam, had gone up to Mount Tabor.
text.KJV: 4:12 And they shewed Sisera, that Barak the sonne of Abinoam was gone vp to mount Tabor.
text.NABRE: 4:12 It was reported to Sisera that Barak, son of Abinoam, had gone up to Mount Tabor.

text.NRSV: Sisera called out all his chariots, nine hundred chariots of iron, and all the troops who were with him, from Harosheth-ha-goiim to the Wadi Kishon.
text.Rotherham: So Sisera called together all his chariots—nine hundred chariots of iron, and all the people that were with him,—from Harosheth of the nations, unto the torrent of Kishon.
text.KJV: 4:13 And Sisera gathered together all his charets, euen nine hundred charets of iron, and al the people that were with him, from Harosheth of the Gentiles, vnto the riuer of Kishon.
text.NABRE: 4:13 So Sisera called out all nine hundred of his iron chariots and all his forces from Harosheth-ha-goiim to the Wadi Kishon.

text.NRSV: Then Deborah said to Barak, “Up! For this is the day on which the Lord has given Sisera into your hand. The Lord is indeed going out before you.” So Barak went down from Mount Tabor with ten thousand warriors following him.
text.Rotherham: Then said Deborah unto Barak—Up! for, this, is the day on which Yahweh hath delivered Sisera into thy hand, hath not, Yahweh, gone forth before thee? So Barak went down from Mount Tabor, with ten thousand men after him.
text.KJV: 4:14 And Deborah said vnto Barak, Up, for this is the day in which the Lord hath deliuered Sisera into thine hand: Is not the Lord gone out before thee? so Barak went downe from mount Tabor, and ten thousand men after him.
text.NABRE: 4:14 Deborah then said to Barak, “Up! This is the day on which the LORD has delivered Sisera into your power. The LORD marches before you.” So Barak went down Mount Tabor, followed by his ten thousand men.

text.NRSV: And the Lord threw Sisera and all his chariots and all his army into a panic before Barak; Sisera got down from his chariot and fled away on foot,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh put to flight Sisera and all the chariots and all the host, with the edge of the sword, before Barak,—so Sisera alighted from his chariot, and fled on foot.
text.KJV: 4:15 And the Lord discomfited Sisera, and all his charets, and all his hoste with the edge of the sword, before Barak: so that Sisera lighted downe off his charet, and fled away on his feet.
text.NABRE: 4:15 And the LORD threw Sisera and all his chariots and forces into a panic before Barak. Sisera himself dismounted from his chariot and fled on foot,

text.NRSV: while Barak pursued the chariots and the army to Harosheth-ha-goiim. All the army of Sisera fell by the sword; no one was left.
text.Rotherham: Now, Barak, pursued the chariots, and the host, as far as Harosheth of the nations,—and all the host of Sisera fell by the edge of the sword, there was not left so much as one.
text.KJV: 4:16 But Barak pursued after the charets, and after the hoste vnto Harosheth of the Gentiles, and all the host of Sisera fell vpon the edge of the sword; and there was not a man left.
text.NABRE: 4:16 but Barak pursued the chariots and the army as far as Harosheth-ha-goiim. The entire army of Sisera fell beneath the sword, not even one man surviving.

text.NRSV: Now Sisera had fled away on foot to the tent of Jael wife of Heber the Kenite; for there was peace between King Jabin of Hazor and the clan of Heber the Kenite.
text.Rotherham: Now, Sisera, had fled on foot, unto the tent of Jael, wife of Heber the Kenite, for there was peace between Jabin, king of Hazer, and the house of Heber the Kenite.
text.KJV: 4:17 Howbeit Sisera fled away on his feet, to the tent of Iael the wife of Heber the Kenite: for there was peace betweene Iabin the king of Hazor, and the house of Heber the Kenite.
text.NABRE: 4:17 Sisera fled on foot to the tent of Jael, wife of Heber the Kenite, for there was peace between Jabin, king of Hazor, and the family of Heber the Kenite.

text.NRSV: Jael came out to meet Sisera, and said to him, “Turn aside, my lord, turn aside to me; have no fear.” So he turned aside to her into the tent, and she covered him with a rug.
text.Rotherham: And Jael went out to meet Sisera, and said unto him: Turn aside, my lord, turn aside with me, do not fear. So he turned aside with her into the tent, and she threw over him a coverlet.
text.KJV: 4:18 ¶ And Iael went out to meete Sisera, and said vnto him, Turne in, my lord, turne in to me, feare not. And when hee had turned in vnto her, into the tent, shee couered him with a mantle.
text.NABRE: 4:18 Jael went out to meet Sisera and said to him, “Turn aside, my lord, turn aside with me; do not be afraid.” So he went into her tent, and she covered him with a rug.

text.NRSV: Then he said to her, “Please give me a little water to drink; for I am thirsty.” So she opened a skin of milk and gave him a drink and covered him.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Let me drink, I pray thee, a little water, for I am thirsty. So she opened the bottle of milk, and gave him to drink, and spread over him the coverlet.
text.KJV: 4:19 And he said vnto her, Giue me, I pray thee, a litle water to drinke, for I am thirstie. And she opened a bottle of milke, and gaue him drinke, and couered him.
text.NABRE: 4:19 He said to her, “Please give me a little water to drink. I am thirsty.” So she opened a skin of milk, gave him a drink, and then covered him.

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Stand at the entrance of the tent, and if anybody comes and asks you, ‘Is anyone here?’ say, ‘No.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her, Stand at the entrance of the tent,—and it shall be, if any man come and ask thee and say—Is there here a man? that thou shalt say, There is not.
text.KJV: 4:20 Againe he said vnto her, Stand in the doore of the tent, and it shall bee when any man doeth come and enquire of thee and say, Is there any man here? that thou shalt say, No.
text.NABRE: 4:20 “Stand at the entrance of the tent,” he said to her. “If anyone comes and asks, ‘Is there someone here?’ say, ‘No!’”

text.NRSV: But Jael wife of Heber took a tent peg, and took a hammer in her hand, and went softly to him and drove the peg into his temple, until it went down into the ground—he was lying fast asleep from weariness—and he died.
text.Rotherham: Then took Jael, wife of Heber, the tent-pin, and put the mallet in her hand, and went in unto him, softly, and smote the tent-pin into his temples, and it pierced through into the ground,—he being fast asleep and shrouded in darkness, and he died.
text.KJV: 4:21 Then Iael Hebers wife, tooke a naile of the tent, and tooke an hammer in her hand, and went softly vnto him, and smote the naile into his temples, and fastened it into the ground: (for he was fast asleepe, and weary;) so he died.
text.NABRE: 4:21 Jael, wife of Heber, got a tent peg and took a mallet in her hand. When Sisera was in a deep sleep from exhaustion, she approached him stealthily and drove the peg through his temple and down into the ground, and he died.

text.NRSV: Then, as Barak came in pursuit of Sisera, Jael went out to meet him, and said to him, “Come, and I will show you the man whom you are seeking.” So he went into her tent; and there was Sisera lying dead, with the tent peg in his temple.
text.Rotherham: And lo! Barak—in pursuit of Sisera! So Jael went forth to meet him, and said unto him, Come and let me shew thee—the man whom thou art seeking. And he came in with her, and lo! Sisera—lying dead, with the tent-pin in his temples.
text.KJV: 4:22 And behold, as Barak pursued Sisera, Iael came out to meet him, and said vnto him, Come, and I will shew thee the man whom thou seekest. And when he came into her tent, behold, Sisera lay dead, and the naile was in his temples.
text.NABRE: 4:22 Then when Barak came in pursuit of Sisera, Jael went out to meet him and said to him, “Come, I will show you the man you are looking for.” So he went in with her, and there lay Sisera dead, with the tent peg through his temple.

text.NRSV: So on that day God subdued King Jabin of Canaan before the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Thus God subdued, on that day, Jabin king of Canaan,—before the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 4:23 So God subdued on that day, Iabin the king of Canaan, before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 4:23 Thus on that day God humbled the Canaanite king, Jabin, before the Israelites;

text.NRSV: Then the hand of the Israelites bore harder and harder on King Jabin of Canaan, until they destroyed King Jabin of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And the hand of the sons of Israel went on, waxing more and more heavy upon Jabin king of Canaan,—until they had cut off Jabin king of Canaan.
text.KJV: 4:24 And the hand of the children of Israel prospered, & preuailed against Iabin the king of Canaan, vntill they had destroyed Iabin king of Canaan.
text.NABRE: 4:24 their power weighed ever more heavily on him, until at length they finished off the Canaanite king, Jabin.

text.NRSV: Then Deborah and Barak son of Abinoam sang on that day, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then sang Deborah, and Barak, son of Abinoam,—on that day, saying:—
text.KJV: 5:1 Then sang Deborah, and Barak the son of Abinoam, on that day, saying,
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 - Song of Deborah. On that day Deborah sang this song—and Barak, son of Abinoam:

text.NRSV: “When locks are long in Israel, when the people offer themselves willingly— bless the Lord!
text.Rotherham: For the leadership of leaders in Israel, For the volunteering of the people,—Bless ye Yahweh!
text.KJV: 5:2 Praise ye the Lord, for the auenging of Israel, when the people willingly offered themselues.
text.NABRE: 5:2 When uprising broke out in Israel, when the people rallied for duty—bless the LORD!

text.NRSV: “Hear, O kings; give ear, O princes; to the Lord I will sing, I will make melody to the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye kings! Give ear, ye princes! I, unto Yahweh,—I, will sing, Will make melody to Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: 5:3 Heare, O ye kings, giue eare, O ye Princes: I, euen I will sing vnto the Lord, I wil sing praise to the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: 5:3 Hear, O kings! Give ear, O princes! I will sing, I will sing to the LORD, I will make music to the LORD, the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: Lord, when you went out from Seir, when you marched from the region of Edom, the earth trembled, and the heavens poured, the clouds indeed poured water.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh! When thou didst come forth out of Seir, When thou didst march along out of the field of Edom, Earth, trembled, Heaven also, poured forth,—Yea, dark clouds, poured forth waters;
text.KJV: 5:4 Lord, when thou wentest out of Seir, when thou marchedst out of the field of Edom, the earth trembled, and the heauens dropped, the clouds also dropped water.
text.NABRE: 5:4 LORD, when you went out from Seir, when you marched from the plains of Edom, The earth shook, the heavens poured, the clouds poured rain,

text.NRSV: The mountains quaked before the Lord , the One of Sinai, before the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: From, mountains, came down streams In presence of Yahweh,—From this Sinai, In presence of Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: 5:5 The mountaines melted from before the Lord, euen that Sinai, from before the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: 5:5 The mountains streamed, before the LORD, the One of Sinai, before the LORD, the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: “In the days of Shamgar son of Anath, in the days of Jael, caravans ceased and travelers kept to the byways.
text.Rotherham: In the days of Shamgar son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the roads were forsaken,—and, the frequenters of highways, betook them to roundabout paths:
text.KJV: 5:6 In the dayes of Shamgar the son of Anath, in the dayes of Iael, the high wayes were vnoccupied, and the traueilers walked thorow by-wayes.
text.NABRE: 5:6 In the days of Shamgar, son of Anath, in the days of Jael, caravans ceased: Those who traveled the roads now traveled by roundabout paths.

text.NRSV: The peasants ceased in Israel, they grew fat on plunder, because you arose, Deborah, arose as a mother in Israel.
text.Rotherham: There was a failure of rulers, in Israel, a failure,—until I arose, a Deborah, arose, a mother in Israel:—
text.KJV: 5:7 The inhabitants of the villages ceased, they ceased in Israel, vntill that I Deborah arose, that I arose a mother in Israel.
text.NABRE: 5:7 Gone was freedom beyond the walls, gone indeed from Israel. When I, Deborah, arose, when I arose, a mother in Israel.

text.NRSV: When new gods were chosen, then war was in the gates. Was shield or spear to be seen among forty thousand in Israel?
text.Rotherham: They chose gods that were new, Then,—war at the gates! Was there, a shield, to be seen? or a spear? among forty thousand in Israel?
text.KJV: 5:8 They chose new gods; then was warre in the gates: was there a shield or speare seene among fourtie thousand in Israel?
text.NABRE: 5:8 New gods were their choice; then war was at the gates. No shield was to be found, no spear, among forty thousand in Israel!

text.NRSV: My heart goes out to the commanders of Israel who offered themselves willingly among the people. Bless the Lord.
text.Rotherham: My heart [saith] —for the governors of Israel, for the volunteers among the people, Bless ye Yahweh!
text.KJV: 5:9 My heart is toward the gouernours of Israel, that offered themselues willingly among the people: Blesse ye the Lord.
text.NABRE: 5:9 My heart is with the leaders of Israel, with the dedicated ones of the people—bless the LORD;

text.NRSV: “Tell of it, you who ride on white donkeys, you who sit on rich carpets and you who walk by the way.
text.Rotherham: Ye that ride on white asses, ye that sit on carpets, and ye that walk upon the road, speak!
text.KJV: 5:10 Speake yee that ride on white asses, yee that sit in Iudgement, and walke by the way.
text.NABRE: 5:10 Those who ride on white donkeys, seated on saddle rugs, and those who travel the road, Sing of them
